cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the amusement park. First, we need to look at all the times Anthony was seen doing various things. The key here is to find out when he wasn't busy, and then check if the amusement park was open during those times. So, Anthony woke up at 6am. Then Leslie saw him at the library reading from 6am to 8am. That means he was occupied there until 8am. Then Jennifer saw him at the gym from 8am to 9am. So after 8am, he was working out. Then Anthony (maybe himself?) saw Anthony at the bakery from 2pm to 9pm. Wait, that's a long time, but that's what it says. Then William saw Anthony buying a bike at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. The park closes after 10pm. Now, the question is, when was Anthony free to go to the amusement park? Let's list the times he was occupied: - 6am - 8am: Library - 8am - 9am: Gym - 2pm - 9pm: Bakery - 9pm - 10pm: Bike shop So after he left the gym at 9am, when was he next seen? The next activity is at 2pm at the bakery. So between 9am and 2pm, he wasn't seen doing anything. That's a free time slot. Let me check again. Wait, the bakery time is from 2pm to 9pm. So from 2pm to 9pm, he was at the bakery. Then after that, he went to the bike shop until 10pm. The amusement park closes at 10pm. So the only time he's not doing anything is between 9am and 2pm. So he could have gone to the park during that time. But wait, the options are (B) 9am to 2pm. But let me double-check if there's any other possible time. The time before 6am is when he woke up, but he was awake at 6am. From 6am to 8am, he was at the library. Then 8-9am at the gym. So 9am is the first free time. Then from 9am to 2pm, he's free. Then from 2pm to 9pm, he's at the bakery. Then 9pm to 10pm at the bike shop. So the amusement park was open until 10pm. But he was at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm.
